content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer array AAA containing NNN elements.

A pair (L,R)(L,R)(L,R),(1≤L<R<N)(1 \le L < R < N)(1≤L<R<N) is called good pair if:


MEX (A[1,⋯L])(A[1, \cdots L])(A[1,⋯L]) === MEX(A[L+1⋯R])(A[L+1 \cdots R])(A[L+1⋯R]) === MEX(A[R+1⋯N])(A[R+1 \cdots N])(A[R+1⋯N])
where MEX of an array denotes the minimal non-negative integer which is not present in that array.

Now Your task is to find both **minimum** and **maximum** value of SUM(A[1⋯L])(A[1 \cdots L])(A[1⋯L]) −-− SUM(A[L+1⋯R])(A[L+1 \cdots R])(A[L+1⋯R]) +++ SUM(A[R+1⋯N])(A[R+1 \cdots N])(A[R+1⋯N]) for any good pair LLL and RRR.

SUMA([X,Y])A([X, Y])A([X,Y]) denotes AX+AX+1+...+AYA_X + A_{X + 1} + ... + A_YAX​+AX+1​+...+AY​.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN - the size of the array.
The second line of each test case contains NNN integers - A1,A2,...,ANA_1, A_2, ..., A_NA1​,A2​,...,AN​.




Output Format
For each test case, print the minimum and maximum value of the expression : SUM(A[1⋯L])(A[1 \cdots L])(A[1⋯L]) −-− SUM(A[L+1⋯R])(A[L+1 \cdots R])(A[L+1⋯R]) +++ SUM(A[R+1⋯N])(A[R+1 \cdots N])(A[R+1⋯N]) for any good pair LLL and RRR.


If no good pair exists, print −1−1-1 -1−1−1.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1 : There is only one good pair L=1,R=2L = 1, R = 2L=1,R=2, and MEX of the 333 parts of the array are all 000. For this pair, the value is 2−4+3=12 - 4 + 3 = 12−4+3=1. This is both the minimum and maximum.
Test Case 2 : It can be proven that there is no good pair in this case.
